verses.text: 'Be ye therefore followers of God... and walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and... given himself for us.' Walk in love. Christ's self-giving is our pattern.
'Be ye therefore followers of God... and walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and... given himself for us.' Walk in love. Christ's self-giving is our pattern.
Let not fornication or covetousness be 'once named among you'; no such hath inheritance in the kingdom. Purity. The kingdom excludes the unclean and greedy.
Let not fornication or covetousness be 'once named among you'; no such hath inheritance in the kingdom. Purity. The kingdom excludes the unclean and greedy.
Let not fornication or covetousness be 'once named among you'; no such hath inheritance in the kingdom. Purity. The kingdom excludes the unclean and greedy.
Let no man deceive you with 'vain words'; be not partakers with them. Beware. Do not be drawn into others' sin.
Let no man deceive you with 'vain words'; be not partakers with them. Beware. Do not be drawn into others' sin.
Ye were darkness, 'but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as children of light.' Changed. The saved walk as children of the day.
Ye were darkness, 'but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as children of light.' Changed. The saved walk as children of the day.
Ye were darkness, 'but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as children of light.' Changed. The saved walk as children of the day.
Have no fellowship with 'the unfruitful works of darkness,' but reprove them; 'Christ shall give thee light.' Expose evil. Light drives out darkness.
Have no fellowship with 'the unfruitful works of darkness,' but reprove them; 'Christ shall give thee light.' Expose evil. Light drives out darkness.
Have no fellowship with 'the unfruitful works of darkness,' but reprove them; 'Christ shall give thee light.' Expose evil. Light drives out darkness.
Have no fellowship with 'the unfruitful works of darkness,' but reprove them; 'Christ shall give thee light.' Expose evil. Light drives out darkness.
Walk circumspectly, 'redeeming the time,' understanding the Lord's will. Use time. The days are evil; spend them wisely.
Walk circumspectly, 'redeeming the time,' understanding the Lord's will. Use time. The days are evil; spend them wisely.
Walk circumspectly, 'redeeming the time,' understanding the Lord's will. Use time. The days are evil; spend them wisely.
'Be not drunk with wine... but be filled with the Spirit'; sing and give thanks. Spirit-filled. Joy in the Spirit fills the heart with praise.
'Be not drunk with wine... but be filled with the Spirit'; sing and give thanks. Spirit-filled. Joy in the Spirit fills the heart with praise.
'Be not drunk with wine... but be filled with the Spirit'; sing and give thanks. Spirit-filled. Joy in the Spirit fills the heart with praise.
Submit one to another; wives, submit to your husbands 'as unto the Lord,' as the church to Christ. Order in love. The home reflects Christ and His church.
Submit one to another; wives, submit to your husbands 'as unto the Lord,' as the church to Christ. Order in love. The home reflects Christ and His church.
Submit one to another; wives, submit to your husbands 'as unto the Lord,' as the church to Christ. Order in love. The home reflects Christ and His church.
Submit one to another; wives, submit to your husbands 'as unto the Lord,' as the church to Christ. Order in love. The home reflects Christ and His church.
'Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it.' Sacrificial love. The husband's love is to be Christlike.
'Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it.' Sacrificial love. The husband's love is to be Christlike.
'Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it.' Sacrificial love. The husband's love is to be Christlike.
Men ought to love their wives 'as their own bodies'; he that loves his wife loves himself. One flesh. Husband and wife are one body.
Men ought to love their wives 'as their own bodies'; he that loves his wife loves himself. One flesh. Husband and wife are one body.
Men ought to love their wives 'as their own bodies'; he that loves his wife loves himself. One flesh. Husband and wife are one body.
A man shall leave father and mother and cleave to his wife; 'this is a great mystery... concerning Christ and the church.' Holy picture. Marriage mirrors Christ's union with His own.
